mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of bus arbitration in a computer system?

options.text: To manage access to a shared bus among multiple potential masters.
To increase the clock speed of the system bus.
To correct data errors that occur during transmission on the bus.
To convert parallel data from the CPU into serial data for the bus.

mcqs.explanation: Bus arbitration is necessary because the bus is a shared resource. Its purpose is to resolve conflicts when multiple devices (e.g., CPU, DMA controller, I/O processors) simultaneously request control of the bus to initiate data transfers.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of the 'daisy-chain' arbitration method?

options.text: It uses a centralized arbiter and a daisy-chained priority signal.
It assigns priority based on the physical distance of a device from the CPU.
It is the fastest and most fair arbitration method for large systems.
It requires the most bus lines compared to other methods.

mcqs.explanation: In daisy-chain arbitration (e.g., using a Bus Grant signal), devices are connected in a serial chain. The arbiter grants the bus to the highest-priority device, which then blocks the grant signal from propagating to lower-priority devices, establishing a fixed priority.

mcqs.question: In a system using independent requests with a parallel priority scheme, what happens when two devices request the bus simultaneously?

options.text: The arbiter's internal logic decodes the requests and grants the bus to the device with the highest pre-assigned priority.
The device physically closest to the arbiter is always granted the bus first.
The bus is granted to each device for alternating clock cycles to ensure fairness.
The requests are queued and processed in the order they were received.

mcqs.explanation: In independent request arbitration, each device has its own request and grant lines to the arbiter. The arbiter uses a priority encoder or similar logic to select the highest-priority requester based on a fixed or programmable scheme.

mcqs.question: What is a major disadvantage of using a simple fixed-priority arbitration scheme?

options.text: It can cause starvation of low-priority devices.
It requires more bus lines than other methods.
It is slower than daisy-chain arbitration.
It cannot handle more than two bus masters.

mcqs.explanation: In a fixed-priority scheme, if higher-priority devices make frequent requests, lower-priority devices may never gain access to the bus, a condition known as starvation.

mcqs.question: Which bus arbitration method is most fault-tolerant because it lacks a single point of failure?

options.text: Distributed (decentralized) arbitration
Centralized daisy-chain arbitration
Centralized independent request arbitration
Polling-based arbitration

mcqs.explanation: Distributed arbitration does not rely on a central arbiter. Each device participates in determining the next bus master, so the system can continue to function even if one device fails.
